bullets.body: **CSAI Foundation announced as MITRE-authorized CVE Numbering Authority (CNA) Apr 29 — first agentic-AI-specific CVE issuer; STAR for AI Catastrophic Risk Annex (4-phase rollout June 2026 → Dec 2027 aligned with NIST AI RMF / EU AI Act / ISO/IEC 42001); AARM specification (contributed by Vanta) + ATF stewardship (transferred from MassiveScale.AI)** — The institutional landing of the agentic-AI threat-class as a first-class disclosure regime, parallel to the Apr 28 ATT&CK v19 named-techniques wave.

citations.label: CSA - CSAI Foundation Announces Key Milestones to Secure the Agentic Control Plane

bullets.body: **MITRE ATT&CK v19 (Apr 28) carry-forward — Defense Evasion (TA0005) splits into Stealth (TA0005) + Defense Impairment (TA0112); v5.4 framework adds AI-agent context poisoning / tool invocation abuse / MCP server compromise / malicious agent deployment as named techniques** — Structural validation of the agent-skills threat-class.

**arxiv 2604.24026 — "From Skill Text to Skill Structure: The Scheduling-Structural-Logical (SSL) Representation for Agent Skills"** — First structured representation for agent skill artifacts. Carry-forward in the skills-as-product wave.

citations.label: arXiv - SSL Representation for Agent Skills

bullets.body: **arxiv 2604.25562 — "SnapGuard: Lightweight Prompt Injection Detection for Screenshot-Based Web Agents"** — Lightweight screenshot-based detector for prompt injection in web agents; targeted at the Cisco-RSAC-prompt-injection 36% baseline.

**arxiv 2604.01194 — "AgentWatcher: A Rule-based Prompt Injection Monitor"** — Output-attribution to causally-influential context segments; addresses the long-context degradation problem in existing detectors.

**arxiv 2604.15368 — "LogJack: Indirect Prompt Injection Through Cloud Logs Against LLM Debugging Agents"** — Cloud logs as a previously-unevaluated indirect-prompt-injection surface; command execution rates 0.0% to 86.2% across model classes.

sections.category: LLM Workflow > OpenClaw / NemoClaw / Hermes Agents

bullets.body: **OpenAI Agents SDK update Apr 30 — model-native harness, native sandbox execution; sandbox providers Blaxel / Cloudflare / Daytona / E2B / Modal / Runloop / Vercel; Python first, TypeScript planned; configurable memory + sandbox-aware orchestration + Codex-like filesystem tools** — Sandbox-execution is now a default primitive across the OpenAI stack with seven third-party sandbox providers as first-class deployment targets.

citations.label: OpenAI - The next evolution of the Agents SDK

bullets.body: **OpenAI Codex CLI 0.128.0-alpha.1 ships Apr 30 — adds `/goal`, in-app browser for local-dev / file-backed pages, Unix-socket app-server transport, sticky environments; model providers own model discovery (AWS/Bedrock account state exposed to app clients); rollout tracing + multi-agent debug reducer; bundled OpenAI Docs skill updated for GPT-5.5 / gpt-image-2** — Developer-side companion to the Apr 28 Bedrock launch.

**Microsoft Agent Framework 1.0 GA (Apr 3) carry-forward + Foundry Toolkit for VS Code GA + Toolbox in Foundry preview + hosted agents in Foundry Agent Service preview + Observability in Foundry Control Plane GA** — The vendor-side answer to the Apr 28 Bedrock AgentCore + Apr 28 Novita Sandbox layer; Agent Framework targets .NET + Python; Foundry Toolkit lives inside VS Code.

citations.label: Microsoft - Microsoft Agent Framework 1.0 inside Foundry

bullets.body: **Novita Sandbox (Apr 28) carry-forward — Firecracker microVM, sub-200ms cold-start, ~1s state-checkpoint resume; OpenClaw + Hermes + autonomous-agent-runtime targets; positioned at Gartner's "55% of AI agent rollouts will be delayed for security reasons"** — Standing reference for the agent-runtime hardening wave; complements the OpenAI Agents SDK Apr 30 + Microsoft Foundry hosted agents Apr 30.

citations.label: PR Newswire - Novita AI Launches Sandbox to Secure OpenClaw, Hermes Agent, and Autonomous Systems

bullets.body: **Hermes Agent v0.11.0 (Apr 23) carry-forward — Ink-based React TUI + Python JSON-RPC backend, native AWS Bedrock, GPT-5.5 via Codex OAuth, 5 new inference paths, 17th messaging platform (QQBot), webhook direct-delivery zero-LLM push, shell-hooks lifecycle integration; 111K+ GitHub stars** — Standing baseline.
**OpenClaw v2026.4.25-beta train continues; 346K+ GitHub stars; NVIDIA NemoClaw early-preview Mar 16 carry-forward into Apr 28-30** — Standing reference for the open-source agentic harness ecosystem.

**Multica v0.2.20 (Apr 29 09:40 UTC) carry-forward into Apr 30-May 1 — open-source vendor-neutral self-hosted orchestrator; superseding v0.2.19 (Apr 28) + v0.2.18 (Apr 27)** — Standing baseline; news-driven coverage per project memory.

**OpenAI on AWS Bedrock launches Apr 28 (limited preview) — GPT-5.5 / OpenAI frontier models on Bedrock; Codex on Bedrock for the 4M+ weekly Codex users; Bedrock Managed Agents powered by OpenAI (built with the OpenAI harness); usage of OpenAI models + Codex applies to existing AWS commitments; full IAM / PrivateLink / guardrails / encryption / CloudTrail integration** — OpenAI's first non-Azure home for first-party model serving; the Apr 27 Microsoft–OpenAI reset is the structural enabling event.

citations.label: AWS - Amazon Bedrock now offers OpenAI models, Codex, and Managed Agents (Limited Preview)

bullets.body: **AWS Bedrock AgentCore Runtime adds Node.js Apr 28 — second supported managed language runtime after Python** — Carry-forward; the agent-runtime layer hardening across AWS + Novita + Microsoft Foundry simultaneously.

citations.label: AWS - Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Runtime now supports Node.js

bullets.body: **AgentCore Managed Harness (4-region preview), CLI (14-region GA, AWS CDK supported), AgentCore Skills on Kiro Power, Claude Cowork in Bedrock** — Carry-forward; the structural baseline that Apr 28 Node.js + Apr 28 OpenAI-on-Bedrock layered on.

citations.label: AWS - Amazon Bedrock AgentCore: New features to build agents faster

sections.category: Local LLM Optimization (Fine-tuning, KV-cache, Inference) > Foundry Local + Local Inference Runtimes

bullets.body: **Microsoft Foundry Local — GA carry-forward (Apr 9 launch); cross-platform (Windows/Linux/macOS), ~20MB native library, native SDKs (C#/JS/Python/Rust), Foundry Catalog hardware-matched model auto-download (GPT-OSS, Qwen, DeepSeek, Mistral, Phi, Whisper), Windows ML (WinML) execution providers; OpenAI request/response format including OpenAI Responses API** — The missing Windows-default integration point for compressed-cache utilities.

sections.category: Local LLM Optimization (Fine-tuning, KV-cache, Inference) > vLLM

bullets.body: **vLLM v0.19.0 (Apr 2) carry into Apr 28-May 1 — torch 2.11 / Python 3.14 / async scheduler default / Model Runner V2 / day-one Gemma 4 support across E2B / E4B / 26B MoE / 31B Dense; v0.18.0 gRPC serving + GPU NGram speculative decoding + FlexKV cache offload backend; HuggingFace Transformers v5+ compatibility; CUDA 13.0 default** — Standing reference.

sections.category: Local LLM Optimization (Fine-tuning, KV-cache, Inference) > SGLang

bullets.body: **SGLang CVE-2026-5760 (CVSS 9.8) — still unpatched into May 1 (~12 days post-disclosure); v0.5.10 ships exploitable; cross-server class to vLLM CVE-2025-61620 + LMDeploy CVE-2026-33626 + Apr 28 Spring AI CVE-2026-40967 + Apr 26 LiteLLM CVE-2026-42208 (same model-tooling-layer injection shape across the stack)** — Maintainer-coordination yields no upstream fix; PoC continues to circulate; CERT/CC VU#915947 active.

**SGLang TurboQuant integration — PR #21419 in-tree (`--kv-cache-dtype turboquant`); follow-up #23134 fused-Triton-decode/extend kernel rewrite hitting 93–105% bf16 throughput vs ~1% on the original implementation** — Standing reference for the compressed-KV-cache deployment-knob trend.

citations.label: GitHub Issue - SGLang #23134 TurboQuant fused Triton kernels

sections.category: Local LLM Optimization (Fine-tuning, KV-cache, Inference) > llama.cpp / Ollama

bullets.body: **llama.cpp build cadence carries into Apr 28-May 1 — sub-day macOS ARM64/x64, Android, OpenEuler, CUDA binaries; TurboQuant discussion thread #20969 active in `ggml-org/llama.cpp`** — Community evaluation of 3.5-bit KV-cache compression on the on-device target.

citations.label: GitHub Discussion - llama.cpp #20969 TurboQuant Extreme KV Cache Quantization

bullets.body: **Ollama v0.21.x carry-forward Apr 28-May 1 — `max` think-parameter + improved OpenAI response mapping for reasoning effort; Hermes + GitHub Copilot CLI integration in `ollama launch`; Gemma 4 MLX text-only runtime** — Standing baseline.

sections.category: Local LLM Optimization (Fine-tuning, KV-cache, Inference) > Unsloth

bullets.body: **Unsloth 2026.4.8 (PyPI) carry-forward into Apr 28-May 1 — inference 20-30% faster (parity with llama-server / llama.cpp); GGUF export expanded to full fine-tunes; auto-detect of LM Studio / HF / similar pre-existing models; faster MoE 30B-A3B (17.5GB VRAM) + 235B-A22B; MoE 12x faster + 35% less VRAM + 6x longer context via new Triton + math kernels with no accuracy loss; Gemma-4-E2B 8GB VRAM training (~1.5x faster, ~60% less VRAM than FA2); new model support DeepSeek-OCR 2 / GLM-4.7-Flash / Kimi-2.5; Unsloth Studio local no-code UI** — Standing on-device fine-tuning baseline alongside Bonsai-8B + TurboQuant; per project memory Unsloth must be searched every daily-update run.

sections.category: Local LLM Optimization (Fine-tuning, KV-cache, Inference) > KV-Cache Compression Stack

bullets.body: **TurboQuant (Google Research, ICLR 2026, arxiv 2504.19874) — 3.5-bit-per-value KV-cache compression matching FP16 on LongBench / Needle-in-a-Haystack with no retraining; 6x compression, 8x faster attention; community ports in MLX + Triton + vLLM + SGLang** — Standing carry-forward.

**PolyKV (arxiv 2604.24971, Apr 27) — shared asymmetrically-compressed KV-pool for multi-agent LLM inference; int8 keys + 3-bit TurboQuant-MSE values via HF DynamicCache injection; on Llama-3-8B with 15 agents sharing 4K-token context cuts KV-cache memory from 19.8GB to 0.45GB (-97.7%)** — Carry-forward.
**NVIDIA kvpress — Decoding-Press maturity; ExpectedAttentionPress with configurable compression ratios (0.7 / 0.5); AIME25 + Math500 + LongBench benchmarks** — Standing baseline.

**DeepSeek V4 Pro / V4 Flash carry-forward Apr 28-May 1; first DeepSeek release optimized for Huawei Ascend chips** — V4 Pro: 1.6T MoE / 49B active; V4 Flash: 284B / 13B active; 1M context; Hybrid Attention reduces compute to 27%/10% of V3.2 in 1M-context. Pricing: V4 Flash $0.14/$0.28 per M tokens; V4 Pro $0.145/$3.48.

**Qwen 3.6 series carry-forward — Qwen3.6-Max-Preview + Qwen3.6-27B + Qwen3.6-35B-A3B; SWE-bench Pro / SkillsBench / SciCode top scores; 260K-token context; preserve_thinking mode for agentic workflows** — Standing baseline.

**GLM-5.1 (Zhipu / Z.AI, Apr 7) carry-forward — top model on SWE-Bench Pro at release, beating GPT-5.4 + Claude Opus 4.6; freely available open-source for complex long-running programming tasks** — Standing reference for the Chinese open-weight coding wave.

bullets.body: **LiteLLM CVE-2026-42208 (CVSS 9.3) — pre-auth SQLi against the Authorization header on /chat/completions; one litellm_credentials row leaks OpenAI org keys + Anthropic admin tokens + AWS Bedrock IAM credentials simultaneously; patched in v1.83.7-stable Apr 19; first observed exploitation Apr 26 16:17 UTC (~26h post-advisory); attackers automated origin-IP rotation 21 minutes apart** — The 2026 LLM-gateway-as-blast-radius case; structurally same shape as Spring AI FilterExpressionConverter (model-tooling-layer injection at the auth/RAG interface).

citations.label: The Hacker News - LiteLLM CVE-2026-42208 SQL Injection Exploited within 36 Hours

bullets.body: **Apr 30 fresh CVE wave — CVE-2026-7503 (CVSS 8.8), CVE-2026-6389 (CVSS 8.8), CVE-2026-6543 (CVSS 8.8), CVE-2026-7551 (CVSS 8.8); UTT HiPER 1200GW strcpy-class router CVEs CVE-2026-7512 + CVE-2026-7513 (CVSS 8.8) disclosed May 1** — Continuing high-severity disclosure cadence.

**CVE-2026-40967 Spring AI FilterExpressionConverter Injection (CVSS 8.6, Apr 28) carry — first 2026 CVE squarely in the LLM-tooling supply chain at the RAG query plane; FilterExpressionConverter accepts a filter-expression object and translates to vector-store query languages without escaping keys/values, enabling query alteration** — Standing carry.
**CVE-2026-3854 GitHub Enterprise Server RCE (CVSS 8.7, Apr 28) carry; CVE-2026-34965 Cockpit CMS Authenticated RCE (CVSS 8.8, Apr 29) carry** — Standing carries from the Apr 28-29 wave.
